front: Film: three-way standoff at Sad Hill Cemetery between Tuco, Angel Eyes, and Blondie, set to Morricone's 'The Ecstasy of Gold'
back: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (Sergio Leone)
work: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (1966)

front: Film: Tuco says 'When you have to shoot, shoot, don't talk' after killing a man who ambushed him in a bathtub
back: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (Sergio Leone)
work: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (1966)

front: Film: protagonist represented by flute theme; Tuco (Eli Wallach) by ocarina version of main theme; set during the American Civil War
back: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (Sergio Leone)
work: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (1966)

front: Film: jarringly cast Henry Fonda as villain Frank; Charles Bronson plays nameless harmonica-playing protagonist seeking revenge for his brother
back: Once Upon a Time in the West (Sergio Leone)
work: Once Upon a Time in the West (1968)

front: Film: opens with a 10-minute mostly silent sequence of three killers waiting at a train station
back: Once Upon a Time in the West (Sergio Leone)
work: Once Upon a Time in the West (1968)

front: Film: Jason Robards plays bandit Cheyenne; Claudia Cardinale plays Jill McBain; land conflict over Brett McBain's dream of building Sweetwater
back: Once Upon a Time in the West (Sergio Leone)
work: Once Upon a Time in the West (1968)

front: Film: plagiarized from Kurosawa's Yojimbo — Leone was sued and settled out of court
work: Dollars Trilogy (A Fistful of Dollars / For a Few Dollars More)

front: Film: opens with a shot of bandit Juan urinating on ants; features a montage of upper-class Mexicans expressing disgust at the poor
work: Duck, You Sucker! / A Fistful of Dynamite (1971)

front: Director: 'Ramen Westerns' (e.g. Tampopo) were named in homage to his Spaghetti Westerns
Director: longtime collaborator with composer Ennio Morricone, who scored all his major Westerns
Director: Clint Eastwood dedicated Unforgiven to this director and Don Siegel, who had cast him in earlier Westerns
